channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Government must urgently fix underfunded public hospitals and restore trust in Kenya's health system
item.description: The article argues that fixing Kenya's public hospitals should be the top priority for the government, given how badly they are currently underfunded and understaffed. The writer points out that many ...

item.title: New Brunswick seeks company to reopen historical antimony mine
item.description: The government of New Brunswick is on the lookout for a company to restart a critical mineral mine, which was once one of the largest producers of antimony in North America. Located about 30 kilometre...

item.title: Tanzania Government Finalises Movable Assets as Collateral for Bank Loans to Boost Credit Access
item.description: The Tanzanian government is in the final stages of rolling out a new system that will let citizens use movable assets like vehicles, equipment, livestock or even inventory as collateral when seeking l...

item.title: How to Fairly Divide Assets After a Creative Partnership Ends
item.description: When a creative partnership dissolves, the division of assets can become complex. The first step is to identify what counts as a shared asset—this includes physical property like equipment and digital...

item.title: Kenyan education stakeholders urge government to prioritise quality over mere access
item.description: The article reports that education stakeholders in Kenya are calling on the government to move beyond simply increasing access to schooling and instead focus on improving the quality of education. The...

item.title: Umahi Orders Court Action Against Tracy Ohiri Over Repeated Allegations of Debt and Harassment
item.description: Minister of Works, Senator David Umahi, don direct im legal team to restart all court cases against Mrs Tracy Ohiri wey dey accuse am of owing her N280 million for campaign materials during im time as...

item.title: Police Commission Takes Strong Action Against Officers for Misconduct, Sanctions AIG Yusuf, and Orders Prosecutions
item.description: The Nigeria Police Service Commission (PSC) has taken significant disciplinary actions against numerous officers of the Nigeria Police Force (NPF), with measures ranging from reprimands to demotions a...

item.title: Eight giraffes moved to Somkhanda reserve to boost genetics and support community conservation
item.description: Eight giraffes have been successfully relocated from Pongola Game Reserve to Somkhanda Community Game Reserve in northern KwaZulu-Natal, aimed at improving genetic diversity and supporting community-d...

item.title: The Glasshouse Theatre in Brisbane: A Stunning Design Featuring Rippling Glass Facade
item.description: In Brisbane, Australia, the newly unveiled Glasshouse Theatre at the Queensland Performing Arts Centre (QPAC) is drawing attention for its stunning, undulating glass façade. Designed by Snøhetta in co...

item.title: World Bank report highlights persistent mismanaged waste despite improving systems
item.description: A new World Bank Group report, What a Waste 3.0, reveals that global municipal solid waste generation reached over 2.6 billion tonnes in 2022 and is projected to increase to 3.9 billion tonnes by 2050...

item.title: Ontario government proposes exemptions from freedom of information requests for premier, cabinet and staff
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford has defended his government's plan to exempt the premier, cabinet ministers, parliamentary assistants and their staff from freedom of information (FOI) laws, granting them ne...

item.title: Adolescence Leads BAFTA TV Nominations with 11 Nods, Including Multiple Acting Mentions
item.description: The Netflix drama 'Adolescence' has taken the lead at the 2026 BAFTA TV Awards with 11 nominations, including five acting nominations for Stephen Graham, Erin Doherty, Owen Cooper, Ashley Walters, and...

item.title: K-Electric names Syed Taha, former PSO chief, as permanent CEO effective April 15
item.description: KARACHI: K-Electric (KE), the power utility serving Karachi, has appointed Syed Taha as its new Chief Executive Officer. The company informed the Pakistan Stock Exchange through an official letter tha...

item.title: Labour law attorney shot dead outside Johannesburg CCMA offices in apparent targeted attack
item.description: A 35-year-old attorney from Krugersdorp, Chinette Gallichan, was tragically shot and killed on Monday morning outside the Commission for Conciliation, Mediation and Arbitration (CCMA) offices at the c...

item.title: DPWH Reports EDSA Rehabilitation Phase One on Schedule for Completion by May
item.description: The Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) announced that the first phase of the EDSA rehabilitation project is progressing well and is expected to be completed by May. According to DPWH Secre...

item.title: Trump's Stance on Iran Deal Amid US Military Escalation in Middle East
item.description: US President Donald Trump has made significant comments regarding the ongoing tensions with Iran. He expressed that Iran is eager to strike a deal, claiming that officials involved in the negotiations...

item.title: Kenya Launches National Earth Observation Data Sharing Framework
item.description: On Monday, the Kenya Space Agency (KSA) officially rolled out the Kenya Earth Observation (EO) Data Sharing Framework 2026, a major step in improving how space-derived data is used across the country....

item.title: Thika Student Builds Own Home Using Profits from Watermelon Farming
item.description: Shalmah, a 22-year-old student at JFC Munene College of Health Sciences in Thika, Kiambu County, has been making waves after constructing homes for both his parents and himself. He first built a KSh 6...

item.title: Moninder Singh Stays Defiant Amid Fresh Assassination Threats in Canada
item.description: Moninder Singh, chairman of the Sikh Federation of Canada, has faced multiple assassination threats, including some against his family, linked to his political activism advocating for an independent S...

item.title: Army and IAF Demonstrate Rapid Deployment of Heavy-Duty Assault Bridge Using Chinook
item.description: In a display of strong coordination between the Indian Army and Air Force, the Army’s combat engineering division, together with the IAF, has successfully deployed a pontoon assault bridge using a Chi...
